items.evidence_summary: The employee could not check in because the partner gym QR scanner failed twice.
items.possible_opportunity: Improve check in reliability and make failed check in recovery clearer for users and partners.

items.evidence_summary: The user did not receive a clear booking confirmation and arrived after the class was already full.
items.possible_opportunity: Make booking confirmation states clearer across the app and follow up messages.

items.evidence_summary: Users searching for yoga near the office saw top results that were too far away or outside commute routes.
items.possible_opportunity: Improve location relevance and commute-aware ranking for class and gym search.

items.evidence_summary: An HR admin asked why only a small share of eligible employees activated the benefit after launch.
items.possible_opportunity: Improve launch communication, activation reminders, and admin visibility into activation blockers.

items.evidence_summary: The benefit became useful only after the user found a nearby gym with no extra fees.
items.possible_opportunity: Surface nearby eligible locations and fee expectations more clearly before the first visit.

items.evidence_summary: The user cancelled because the cancellation policy was unclear and they did not want to be charged.
items.possible_opportunity: Show cancellation rules at booking time and before cancellation decisions.

items.evidence_summary: Employees understood the benefit better after a launch reminder, but usage still dropped after the first week.
items.possible_opportunity: Create after launch nudges that help employees find a relevant first activity and build repeat usage.

recommended_review_questions: Which partners or devices account for the highest share of failed check ins?
Where in the booking flow do users lose confidence that a class is confirmed?
Are low activation and first week usage drops concentrated by employer, location, or launch communication pattern?
